programme.title: Xploration Awesome Planet S6 - "Wind"
programme.desc: In this episode, Philippe takes us inside one of the most powerful forces on Earth - wind.

programme.title: Xploration Awesome Planet S6 - "Student Explorer"
programme.desc: In this episode, we reveal the winner of our third ever #StudentExplorer Contest.

programme.title: Xploration Awesome Planet S6 - "Sustainable Leaders"
programme.desc: In this episode, we’ll visit a facility in Reno that makes biofuel for United Airlines aircraft.

programme.title: Angry Planet S2 - "Hot and Cold Crystals"
programme.desc: Miners in Chihauhau, Mexico, has just discovered an amazing sight. The Mexican authorities are only opening up the cavern to scientists and film crews – “Angry Planet” plans to be one of the first in!

programme.title: Angry Planet S2 - "Thunder Down Under"
programme.desc: In the first week of December – the height of storm season in Australia, we plan to travel with Jimmy Deguara, the most experienced of Australian stormchasers.

programme.title: Angry Planet S2 - "Hawaii"
programme.desc: We have filmed extensively in Hawaii, filming big-wave surfing at the famous Banzai Pipeline on Oahu’s North Shore, and filming George surfing with the “Hawaian Fire” firefighters. We have also filmed at the Hawaii Tsunami Institute and in Hilo.

programme.title: Angry Planet S2 - "Italia Wild"
programme.desc: Italy is one of the very few nations in the world that regularly gets three major extreme natural phenomena – volcanos, flooding, and avalanches. The most recent eruption was in November 2006.

programme.title: Skuld: A Look into the Future S1 - "Energy Globe Award 21st - Part 2"
programme.desc: Watch the second part of a feature on the second award ceremony for sustainability projects that can change the world.

programme.title: Skuld: A Look into the Future S1 - "Logistic 4.0"
programme.desc: Follow the parcel worldwide delivery technology.

programme.title: Skuld: A Look into the Future S1 - "Energy Globe Award - Part 1"
programme.desc: Follow the feature about the 2022 award ceremony for sustainability projects that can change the world.

programme.title: Wild Faces of Switzerland S1 - "The Jura Mountains"
programme.desc: The ibex, marmot, and European adder have all developed strategies to survive the alpine winter.

programme.title: Wild Faces of Switzerland S1 - "Ticino"
programme.desc: Learn about four different regions of Switzerland. From the rugged mountains of the Ticino High Alps to the lovely Muggio Valley in the southernmost corner of Ticino, hardly any other canton is so full of contrasts.

programme.title: Wild Faces of Switzerland S1 - "Water and Ice"
programme.desc: The river called Aare is the only body of water that runs through Switzerland. It rises from a source in the Bernese Alps and finally flows into the Rhine. The Aere offers an important habitat for flora and fauna.

programme.title: Super Senses S1 - "Hearing"
programme.desc: On this episode we travel from the desert, to the forest, Australia to Borneo and back to learn how species as diverse as Bat-eared Foxes, the Bilby, Deer, Kangaroo Rats, cicadas, Proboscis Monkeys and other creatures never miss an auditory trick.

programme.title: Wildest India S1 - "Thar Desert : Sacred Sands"
programme.desc: Covering 125,000 square miles, India's Thar desert is one of the harshest places on Earth. Desiccating winds and a near-permanent drought has earned this unforgiving land another name: the region of death.

programme.title: Wildest India S1 - "Ganges: River of Life"
programme.desc: The Ganges is the longest river in India. It flows from the glaciers of the Himalayas all the way down to the Bay of Bengal. It's a lifeline to wild animals, from rhinoceros and river dolphins to otters and gharial crocodiles.

programme.title: Wildest India S1 - "The Himalayas: Surviving the Summits"
programme.desc: The Himalayas run across 1550 miles of north India. The Himalayas' rugged highlands offer little direct refuge to humans, and in the shadow below, over a billion people in India rely on the mountains for survival.

programme.title: Wildest India S1 - "Western Ghats: Monsoon Mountains'"
programme.desc: The Western Ghats are a spine of mountains that lay claim to being one of the most biodiverse places in the world. In the east, tigers and wild dogs compete for prey. All the while, the western Ghats hold the key to life across southern India.

programme.title: Amazing Animals S1 - "Wild No More Part 1"
programme.desc: Wild No More shows the differences between the animals that share our habitat and their counterparts in the wild.

programme.title: Amazing Animals S1 - "Wild No More Part 2"
programme.desc: Wild No More shows the differences between the animals that share our habitat and their counterparts in the wild.

programme.title: Breakthrough S1 - "Shark Family Tree"
programme.desc: Great white sharks are one of the most feared predators on Earth, and yet we know very little about them. Science is now revealing the mysteries of this perfect predator through evolutionary biology.

programme.title: Xploration Earth 2050 S2 - "Drones"
programme.desc: The sky is the limit as host Chuck Pell discovers how drone technology is saving the lives of thousands in disaster sites around the world.

programme.title: Xploration Earth 2050 S2 - "Future Law Enforcement"
programme.desc: Host Chuck Pell experiences a forger’s nightmare when his handwriting is analyzed with the latest computer software.

programme.title: Xploration Earth 2050 S2 - "The Future Explores The Past"
programme.desc: Host Chuck Pell sees how archeologists are using satellite imaging and 3D sensing to map ancient native American cities.

programme.title: Xploration Earth 2050 S2 - "The Fascinating Future Of Growing Food"
programme.sub-title: The Fascinating Future Of Growing Food
programme.desc: Host Chuck Pell introduces us to developments going on in labs, research farms, plant genetics and something called aquaponics to enhance our ability to grow food.

programme.title: Sci Q S1 - "Sci Quiz"
programme.desc: Fast-paced, pop-science show that will test your science I.Q. How long can a person exist without water? How fast is the International Space Station travelling through space? What exactly are DNA Forensics?

programme.title: Sci Q S1 - "Sci IQ"
programme.desc: Fast-paced, pop-science show that will test your science I.Q. On average, how deep are our oceans? What was the largest earthquake ever measured on the Richter scale? What is an apex predator?

programme.title: Sci Q S1 - "Sci World"
programme.desc: Fast-paced, pop-science show that will test your science I.Q. What’s the hairiest animal on the planet? What is the most common cause of the waves in the ocean? Why does the surface of Mars look red?

programme.title: Sci Q S1 - "Pop Sci Test"
programme.desc: Fast-paced, pop-science show that will test your science I.Q. Do you know what causes an avalanche? What is the lightest element in the universe, hydrogen or helium? What was the main purpose of the Space Shuttle Program?

programme.title: Quick Connections with Alie Ward S1 - "Engineering Earth"
programme.desc: A heroic bid for freedom by an enslaved couple in 19th century America precipitates great innovation that culminates in people literally making heavens open. But can controlling the weather save us from climate catastrophe? Or are we playing god?

programme.title: Quick Connections with Alie Ward S1 - "Becoming Bionic"
programme.desc: From dissecting stolen corpses and peering into microscopes, the secret structure of the brain and nervous system is exposed. Could this lead to a race of superhumans?

programme.title: Xploration DIY Sci S6 - "Believe It Or Not"
programme.desc: Host Steve Spangler presents amazing science stunts that your friends will not believe – but they’re real! – from pre-sliced bananas to the way to crush raw eggs without turning them to mushy egg soup.

programme.title: Xploration DIY Sci S6 - "Science In The Kitchen"
programme.desc: Steve Spangler creates science toys, tricks and experiments that you can make using simple things you can find in your kitchen.

programme.title: Xploration DIY Sci S6 - "The DIY Sci Challenge"
programme.desc: One group of fourth grade students faces “The DIY SCI Challenge.” Steve Spangler challenges them to invent a contraption that will keep an egg from breaking when it’s dropped from a ladder.

programme.title: Xploration DIY Sci S6 - "Recycled Science"
programme.desc: Steve Spangler shows you how to take your recycled junk and turn it into the coolest do-it-yourself science.

programme.title: Oddly Satisfying Science S1 - "Science on Fire"
programme.desc: This week's experiments investigate how you can burn metal using only a 9 volt battery, what fire can teach us about calories, and why a little bit of sodium can cause a gigantic explosion.

programme.title: Oddly Satisfying Science S1 - "Invisible Impacts"
programme.desc: This week's experiments explore the resonant frequencies of bridges and human bodies, the mind boggling world of quantum levitation, and how to whip up the conditions to recreate nature's scariest cyclone: a fire tornado.

programme.title: Oddly Satisfying Science S1 - "Water, Water Everywhere"
programme.desc: In this week's experiments we discover how water can start a fire, transform breakfast cereal into a magnetic field detector, and even change the way you visualize the world around you.
